world.title: Tales of Tasern
world.core_premise: A world at the edge of existence where belief shapes reality.
world.summary: Tasern is an Earth-scale fantasy world influenced by the Flow of Existence, a metaphysical current that moves outward from the Pillars of Creation. Reality becomes thinner toward the edge, belief can shape gods and nations, and travel is generally slow except through rare gates and elite sky travel.
themes: slow travel across a vast world
civilizations rise and fall through memory, faith, and fear
public myth and private truth are both world-shaping forces

suns: One sun is a gateway to endless fire.
One sun is described as a castle of light.
moons: White moon tied to white dragons that consume everything, even each other.
Green moon tied to deadly forests.
Blue moon tied to endless seas and aerial-capable sealife.

cosmology.pillars_of_creation: Unreachable source from which new souls enter existence.

souls.origin: New souls enter from the Pillars of Creation.
souls.conservation: Souls are generally conserved unless annihilated.
afterlife_logic: Souls may reincarnate or transform into beings such as angels, devils, or demons.
Plane collapse can destabilize connected chains of existence.

devils_and_demons.devils: Lawful protectors that prevent demons from escaping the ring.
devils_and_demons.demons: Agents of corruption, destruction, and possible annihilation.

magic_distribution.Elpha: High and widely taught magic with formal schools.
magic_distribution.Skrim: High and widely taught magic with strong institutional use.
magic_distribution.Durgan Dynasty: Mid-magic culture with academies and organized wizardry.

social_patterns: Humans are generally the most broadly accepting.
Igypt is among the most culturally accepting, but judges by contribution and sacrifice.
Hostility varies heavily by region and situation.
Gates and sky travel are rare for common people and mostly controlled by elites or adventurers.

Durgan Dynasty.description: A powerful human realm with a 500-year unbroken ruling line and a founding maxim roughly summarized as 'don't kill humans.' Known for flying ships, markets, merchant houses, wizardry, and immense urban scale.

capital_region.Durganthrone: Flying castle-city and current capital hovering above Durganhold.
capital_region.First Wall: Ancient defensive perimeter and shrine to human civilization.
capital_region.Tribesmeet: Original castle and hall of congress.
capital_region.Iron Rise: Rocky overlook with an ancient watchtower.

Elpha.description: Realm of long-lived elves, master enchanters, and formal magical excellence. Ruled by the pureblood emperor known as The Eldest One, whose authority is tied to belief.
Elpha.capital: Vaelthiryaen, the Emerald and Amber Palace
notes: Pure bloodlines can stretch back ten thousand years.
Long war and distrust toward Orklin.

Argenti.description: Ancient, long-lived dwarves known for endurance, isolation, and master craftsmanship. They create works meant to outlast ages and hold deep traditions around stone and memory.
beliefs: Some traditions emphasize a return to stone.

Orklin.description: A low-magic society undergoing industrial transformation with steam power, steel war machines, and practical strength-through-industry values.
Orklin.leadership_notes: Long-lived gnomes secretly guide multi-generational ork rulers.

Igypt.type: Desert civilization under a god-king pharaoh
Igypt.description: A grand desert power that idolizes those who give of themselves eternally and despises those who take for themselves eternally. Includes both noble and corrupt preserved dead.
traits: Strong emphasis on sacrifice and service

Skrim.description: A nation run with corporate efficiency under Mr. Carpenter, with cathedral power, gate-linked logistics, and deep institutional control.

Pirates of the Thousand Kingdoms.type: Decentralized sea and off-world trader network
Pirates of the Thousand Kingdoms.description: A loose web of maritime and extraplanar traders who shape commerce, rumor, culture, and movement through the Isles and beyond.

Dragons.description: Color- and clan-divided dragons that can remain ageless if sufficiently fed, often ruling through tribute.

Druids.description: Guardians of growth and decay who preserve balance, sometimes through terrible means such as plague or famine.

regions_and_landmarks.Red Spire: An ancient fire dragon opened a portal to the fire realm here, creating the Red Wastes.
regions_and_landmarks.Blue Barrens: Eastern lands of lightning dragons and energy-harvesting cultures.
regions_and_landmarks.White Wastes: Frozen reaches associated with white dragons and consumption.

Glowing Grove.description: A large regional biome and breeding ground for Aethertitans. It includes coasts, settlements, migratory routes, and repeated catastrophic convergence events.
notes: Not limited to the turtles themselves.
Supports multiple story settings including coastal cities.
Convergence events reshape land and memory.

Luminocity.description: A thousand-year-old dwarven settlement in the Glowing Grove that survives repeated convergence events through natural sandbars and constructed sea walls.
Luminocity.importance: Preserves more continuity than most places in the region, though still lacks full understanding of the disasters.

aethertitans.name: Wandering Shells
aethertitans.description: Continent-scale turtles that carry island chains and generate Aetherreef ecosystems. Their movements influence ocean currents, atmospheric currents, and even the behavior of the Flow.

convergence.summary: Rare mating events, often associated with moon alignment on roughly 700-year cycles, causing catastrophic floods, earthquakes, and falling land where shells overlap.

male_vs_female_shells.female_shells: Near-total surface resets; cultures must be highly adaptive and rarely persist.
male_vs_female_shells.male_shells: Partial continuity is possible, but still subject to severe collapse and fragmentation.

aethertitans.knowledge_problem: Knowledge preservation systematically fails across convergence cycles. Female shells reset almost entirely. Male shells preserve fragments, but continuity is broken by time, conflict, and disaster, causing cyclical rediscovery.

Verdant Accord.description: A movement opposing spine carving and warning that recent carving practices are tied to natural disasters.
Verdant Accord.leader: Liora Venn, called the First Voice

Thornpath.description: An extremist offshoot that believes violent disruption is necessary to end spine carving.
Thornpath.leader: Serra Thorne, called The Unyielding Root
Thornpath.leader_notes: A fan of Liora Venn who has never met her. Serra plans to collapse spine-carved settlements, sacrificing herself and likely many others to prevent future generations from suffering what she sees as normalized brutality.

loyalists.description: Humans living in dwarven settlements. They are useful but treated as second-class residents, especially during convergence triage.
loyalists.tension: Dwarves and gnomes reproduce slowly and often prioritize preserving key members, producing conflict between practical triage factions and resentful human populations.

major_threats.Grandfather: A parasitic cosmic hive entity and one of the existential threats to the world.
major_threats.Hoard Mother: An ancient white dragon in the Icey Waste.

Crystal Lady.description: A death-linked manipulator capable of manifesting anywhere. She is no longer primarily tied to Igypt, though her stronghold between Igypt and Argenti disrupts trade routes.
traits: Can heal and harm with death magic
Uses blood and souls of others to fuel dark power
Can infiltrate heroic groups for long periods before betrayal
May appear as a child, young woman, or old lady

Elderglen.description: A fast-growing valley hub tied to the gate at Rockridge. Population rose from under 100 to around 10,000 within months, creating major tensions in supply, power, and identity.
features: Undead breaches tied to magical research

notable_figures.Lexa Bella: Guardian and recruitment figure, often used to bring players into the story.
notable_figures.Gary: Gareth Alderwood, local king-like authority with familiar style.
notable_figures.Frank: Wizard academy figure tied to interdimensional skepticism and dangerous inquiry.
notable_figures.Darkstar: Dark elf paladin of the sun and a strong stabilizing ally.

Elderglen.adventure_hook: Escort supplies from Rockridge to Elderglen as the valley strains under explosive growth.

usage_notes_for_ai.tone: Epic fantasy with layered myth, political tension, and metaphysical stakes.
style_guidance: Treat belief, rumor, and public perception as forces that can materially shape the world.
Remember that travel is slow and logistics matter unless gates or elite sky travel are involved.
Preserve faction identities and internal contradictions.
When uncertain, prefer mystery over contradiction.
